Baselines

Text generation

Different models and parameters

    temperature=0.3,         # Override temperature just for this call

    max_new_tokens=50,

    top_p=0.9

Do sample False - makes it repeat a lot

Top_k small also makes it repeat( limits to top k next words

Top_p having it low also makes it repeat. (limits the smallest set of workds that have that probability.

- max_new_tokens: Number of new tokens to generate (see above).
- max_length: Total length of input + generated tokens.
- temperature: Float (e.g., 0.7). Lower is more focused/predictable, higher is more random.
- do_sample: Bool. Whether to use sampling or greedy/beam search.
- top_k: Integer. Limits sampling to the top K most likely next tokens.
- top_p: Float (0-1). Nucleus sampling—sample from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability is > top_p.
- repetition_penalty: Float (e.g., 1.2). Penalizes repeated phrases.
- num_return_sequences: Integer. How many completions to generate per prompt.
- num_beams: Integer. Activates beam search for higher-quality but more deterministic outputs.
- early_stopping: Bool. Stop generation when all beams generate an end token.
- no_repeat_ngram_size: Integer. Prevents repetition of n-grams of this size.
- pad_token_id / eos_token_id: For customizing special tokens.
- return_full_text: Whether to return the prompt plus output, or just the output.

- min_length / min_new_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate.
- bad_words_ids / force_words_ids: Prevent or force certain words in output.
- length_penalty: Used with beam search to favor longer/shorter outputs.
- decoder_start_token_id: For encoder-decoder models.
- truncate / truncation: Cut off prompt if too long.
- seed: Set for reproducibility.
- output_scores: Return scores/probabilities.
